round2_improvement_notes.linguistic: Agent provided no output in either round; no improvement possible.
round2_improvement_notes.landscape: Agent acknowledged other agents' findings (Ficus religiosa) and refined confidence levels, though the conclusion remained the same.
round2_improvement_notes.botanics: Agent synthesized cross-domain evidence (roof tiles, auto-rickshaw) to strengthen its botanical argument, showing good synthesis.
round2_improvement_notes.regulatory: Agent integrated landscape and botanics evidence to support its regulatory findings, demonstrating synthesis.
round2_improvement_notes.meta: Agent incorporated landscape and botanics findings to reinforce its meta-level synthesis.

role_leakage_notes.botanics: Minor leakage: Referenced 'Mangalore-style roof tiles' (landscape/architecture) and 'auto-rickshaw model' (regulatory/meta) to support botanical claims.
role_leakage_notes.regulatory: Minor leakage: Referenced 'architectural style' (landscape) and 'orange religious bunting' (meta) to support regulatory claims.

verdict.judge_synthesis_notes: The judge correctly synthesized the evidence, noting the linguistic agent's failure to provide input. It effectively weighted the strong visual markers (Mangalore tiles, auto-rickshaw, Ficus religiosa, orange flags) to confirm India, while explicitly ruling out Nepal and Pakistan based on the specific combination of these factors.
